reasoning: Avatar 3 (Fire and Ash) is highly unlikely to win Best Picture. Historically, blockbuster sequels (like the Avatar films) face an extreme uphill battle for the top prize, with rare exceptions like 'The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King'. While it stands a fair chance at a Best Picture nomination due to the expanded 10-film field, winning generally requires substantial above-the-line support (Acting, Directing, Screenplay), which the Avatar franchise has lacked. 'Avatar: The Way of Water' secured only 4 nominations and won only for Visual Effects. Consequently, there is almost no conceivable path for Avatar 3 to command the broad Academy consensus required to actually win Best Picture. Combining this with a non-zero probability of release date delays, the likelihood of an 'Avatar 3' Best Picture win at the 98th Academy Awards realistically sits below 2%. Therefore, I am predicting the minimum allowed probability of 0.02.
